actionUnits.condition: Furniture (fileID: 237) has no Rigidbody, so only Trigger actions are applicable. Execute manual UnityEvent invocations if and only if: (a) a Collider (isTrigger or collision-based) exists on Furniture or its children, (b) an EventTrigger component exists, or (c) a MonoBehaviour on Furniture/children exposes UnityEvents (m_Calls) or public no-arg methods. The provided scene excerpt does not include any MonoBehaviour, Collider, or EventTrigger components for Furniture; therefore enumerate and invoke those once their script_fileID and m_Calls are known.

EventTrigger (script_fileID: 994, guid f70555f1...) on default(fileID: 264, tag: mirror) has delegate eventID 0 mapped to Collectable.SetGazedAt(true) on MonoBehaviour Collectable (script_fileID: 995, guid fd9b592f...). This simulates gaze enter/highlight. BoxCollider (id: 794) present enables interaction. MonoBehaviour settings in scene match the attached Collectable script (inactiveMaterial, gazedAtMaterial, dispatch).

EventTrigger (script_fileID: 994) delegate eventID 1 mapped to Collectable.SetGazedAt(false) on Collectable (script_fileID: 995). This simulates gaze exit/unhighlight. Scene MonoBehaviour configuration corresponds to the Collectable.SetGazedAt(bool) call in the script.

Use (click/activate) path: EventTrigger (script_fileID: 994) delegate eventID 4 invokes Usable.Use(Inventory inv= fileID: 985) on MonoBehaviour Usable (script_fileID: 996, guid 164fdbff...). Failure/inspect branch: when Inventory.SelectedItem == null or != useItem (as serialized in scene), Usable.Inspect() or Inspect(failureMessage) is executed. Usable MonoBehaviour in scene has dispatch UnityEvent wired to FindEscape.Escape (script_fileID: 997) but only fires on success; this case verifies failure messaging. Scene MonoBehaviour settings match Usable/Inspectable scripts (inspectMessage, dispatch persistent call).

Use success branch: Precondition Inventory.SelectedItem == Usable.useItem (guid 3ecd47c1...). EventTrigger (script_fileID: 994) delegate eventID 4 invokes Usable.Use(inv) on Usable (script_fileID: 996), which on success invokes its dispatch UnityEvent, persistently mapped to FindEscape.Escape() on MonoBehaviour FindEscape (script_fileID: 997, guid 039fb840...). This covers the escape sequence that manipulates objects by tag 'mirror' and 'digitalLED', disables components, and stops audio.

Direct pickup path to cover Collectable.Collect(): Collectable (script_fileID: 995) exposes a public no-arg Collect() that adds item to Inventory (if assigned) and invokes its dispatch UnityEvent. Scene shows Collectable.dispatch with no persistent calls; calling Collect() still validates destruction path and event safety.

Gaze enter highlight via EventTrigger on vase. MonoBehaviour id 1010 (UnityEngine.EventSystems.EventTrigger, guid f70555f1...) on default(fileID: 277, tag: vase) has delegate eventID 0 mapped to Collectable.SetGazedAt(true) on MonoBehaviour id 1009 (Collectable, guid fd9b592f...). BoxCollider id 798 enabled, so trigger is valid. Scene MonoBehaviour fields (inactiveMaterial, gazedAtMaterial, dispatch) match Collectable script.
Gaze exit unhighlight via EventTrigger on vase. EventTrigger (id 1010) delegate eventID 1 mapped to Collectable.SetGazedAt(false) on MonoBehaviour id 1009 (Collectable).

Use (activate) failure/inspect branch. EventTrigger (id 1010) delegate eventID 4 invokes Usable.Use(Inventory inv=fileID: 985) on MonoBehaviour id 1008 (Usable, guid 164fdbff...). Precondition: Inventory.SelectedItem == null or != Usable.useItem. Expected: Usable.Inspect() or Inspect(failureMessage). This covers Usable path without success dispatch. Scene components confirm Usable is the target of eventID 4.

Use (activate) success branch leading to placing the flower. EventTrigger (id 1010) delegate eventID 4 invokes Usable.Use(inv=fileID: 985) on MonoBehaviour id 1008 (Usable). Precondition: Inventory.SelectedItem == Usable.useItem. ReplaceFlower script is attached as MonoBehaviour id 1011 (guid b9a7c98e...).

Directly exercise Collectable.Collect() on vase to cover pickup/destruction path and UnityEvent safety. MonoBehaviour id 1009 matches Collectable script (guid fd9b592f...), dispatch has no persistent calls in scene.

Inspectable (MonoBehaviour id: 909, guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) is attached to default (fileID: 57) and serialized with inspectMessage 'This looks out of place...'. Calling Inspect() covers Inspect() -> Inspect(string) path.

Collectable (MonoBehaviour id: 911, guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) on default (fileID: 57) has UnityEvent 'dispatch' persistently mapped to MovePillow.MovePillowAway() (MonoBehaviour id: 910, guid: 85f9854fdebef48de893bf8e6be7bb91). BoxCollider (id: 778) present; components may be disabled at runtime by MovePillow.Start(), but direct invocation ensures coverage. Calling Collect() triggers dispatch -> MovePillowAway(), which translates the pillow, sets isMoved, and enables the key collider.

default (fileID: 27) under Furniture has BoxCollider (id: 776, isTrigger: 0) and three MonoBehaviours configured as follows in the scene: Collectable (MonoBehaviour id: 898, script guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) with dispatch.m_Calls empty and item null; Inspectable (MonoBehaviour id: 899, script guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) with inspectMessage ''; Lightable (MonoBehaviour id: 900, script guid: 88bc783c768ce4eb29e7f2d95dc4f793). Lightable requires a child Light component to be present; ToggleLight updates Inspectable.inspectMessage when toggled. Trigger is valid due to the Collider on this object.

Scene meta confirms grp2 (fileID: 195, parent: Mid Drawer) has BoxCollider (id: 786, isTrigger: 0) enabling Trigger actions. It has MonoBehaviour id: 971 bound to script guid: 311424da740a844ab963ccaad56ef8b6, which matches AnimateDrawer. This script exposes Start() and OpenDrawer() (both public, no params). Start sets origin and reparents child 'Knife' to the drawer's parent; OpenDrawer translates the drawer and disables Usable, Collectable, and Collider on this object. Note: If Usable/Collectable are not present on grp2, OpenDrawer() will attempt to access null components and could throw; ensure they exist or expect a NullReference during testing.

Scene meta confirms Knife (fileID: 265) has BoxCollider (id: 795, isTrigger: 0) enabling Trigger actions. It has: Collectable (MonoBehaviour id: 999, script guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) with item set, inactive/gazed materials assigned, dispatch.m_Calls empty; Inspectable (MonoBehaviour id: 1000, script guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) with a serialized inspectMessage. An EventTrigger component is present and serialized to call Collectable.SetGazedAt(true) on pointer enter (eventID: 0). This action simulates pointer enter to execute that EventTrigger call.
Simulate pointer exit (EventTrigger eventID: 1) to invoke Collectable.SetGazedAt(false) per scene meta m_Calls, exercising the other branch of SetGazedAt.

Simulate pointer click (EventTrigger eventID: 4). Scene meta m_Calls map click to Collectable.Collect() (id: 999) then Inspectable.Inspect() (id: 1000). Collect will add the configured item to Inventory and schedule this GameObject for destruction at end of frame; Inspect will show the serialized message.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 931) in scene meta is the Collectable script (guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) attached to GameObject 'default' (fileID: 108). It references item (guid: a874fb11274d84eb3b6f5c630630c27a), inactiveMaterial (guid: 28db40d0099852448a1816652a486146), gazedAtMaterial (guid: 4ad709961269449e38e60dcaa32e102e), and dispatch UnityEvent with m_Calls=[ ] (no persistent listeners). CapsuleCollider (id: 812) present, so Trigger is valid. Call Collect() last because it adds the item to Inventory then destroys this GameObject and invokes dispatch (no listeners).

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 981) is ClockTimer (script guid: e7961a963e9940a44b4f5cf35ce83696) attached to GameObject 'TableClock Type3' (fileID: 230). Scene meta shows fields: initialHoursValue=0, initialMinutesValue=0, initialSecondsValue=0, clockSpeed=1, pointFlicker=true, reverse=false. ClockTimer exposes no public no-arg methods; its coverage-relevant callbacks are OnEnable() and Update(). To cover OnEnable, the component or GameObject must be toggled from disabled to enabled; to cover Update, the object must remain enabled for at least one frame. However, this GameObject has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger and the script does not derive from UnityEngine.Events, so a test-time Trigger interaction is only valid if: (a) a Collider/EventTrigger exists on this object or its interaction policy allows lifecycle toggling, or (b) you authorize enabling/disabling the component/GameObject during the test. This step requests toggling the enabled state to invoke OnEnable, then waiting triggerring_time to exercise Update’s assignment of digitalClock.clockSpeed.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 942) in scene meta is the DigitalClock script (guid: 21b908ce4d9cbc14489c84c042cae40b) attached to GameObject 'DigitalClock2_LED' (fileID: 138). Its serialized fields map as: hoursDCV=925, minutesDCV=913, secondsDCV=null, pointsDCV=943, pointFlicker=true, clockSpeed=1. DigitalClock.Start() auto-starts PlayTime and, because secondsDCV is null and pointFlicker=true, also PlayPointFlicker. GameObject 'default' (fileID: 167, tag: 'clock') under the same TableClock has a BoxCollider (id: 784) and an Inspectable component (id: 954), satisfying Trigger constraints and serving as the target for DigitalClock’s runtime update to Inspectable.inspectMessage. Wait 61 seconds to cover: (1) PlayPointFlicker’s 1-second tick loop, and (2) PlayTime’s else-branch (secondsDCV==null) with a 60-second wait, which calls minutesDCV.ChangeTimeValue(), updates the tagged 'clock' Inspectable message to 'It reads HH:MM.', and sets DigitalClock.score = hours*60 + minutes.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 925) on GameObject 'Hours' (fileID: 92) uses script guid 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec, which corresponds to DigitalClockValue. LED Yellow Numbers (fileID: 29) has MonoBehaviour_0 id: 901 with script guid 1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9, which is DisplaySelectedObject. Scene settings match the script fields: displayOptions=[126,134,52,203,67,259,286,185,182,113], currentDisplay=0, temporalGOArrange=0, ButtonSelectable=false. OnEnable will auto-run at scene load (temporalGOArrange==0), calling TurnOffAll(), then activating displayOptions[currentDisplay] and setting oldDisplay=0. To cover DisplaySelectedObject.TurnSelectedWithButton both branches (curDisp==oldDisplay and curDisp!=oldDisplay) without direct parameter calls, drive it via parent 'Hours' DigitalClockValue (MonoBehaviour id: 925, guid 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec). Invoking ChangeTimeValue() increments currentValue and calls tens.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue/10). Starting at 0, one call yields tens=0 (equals oldDisplay); after ten total calls, tens=1 (not equal), exercising both paths. No UnityEvent m_Calls are defined on id: 901.

MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 913) on GameObject 'Minutes' (fileID: 65) uses script guid 99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec, which matches DigitalClockValue. Trigger ChangeTimeValue 60 times to cover: (1) normal increment path (!reverse), (2) rollover branch when currentValue becomes 60 (> maximumValue-1) resetting to 0, and (3) NextValue!=null cascade invoking Hours.ChangeTimeValue() once. Each call will also exercise tens.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue/10) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue%10) on their DisplaySelectedObject components.

To achieve full functional coverage of TurnSelectedWithButton(int) across indices 0..9, we need either: (a) the parent 'Minutes' GameObject's script (e.g., a clock incrementer) script_fileID and a parameterless method (e.g., ChangeTimeValue) that invokes TurnSelectedWithButton on this component, or (b) an EventTrigger/UnityEvent (m_Calls) on this or a related GameObject configured to call TurnSelectedWithButton with an int argument. Provide the 'Minutes' MonoBehaviour component details (script_fileID, method name) or any UnityEvent m_Calls that pass the required int. If temporalGOArrange=1 is ever used in-scene, we also need a configuration or method to toggle it to cover TemporalMethodArrangeModels/TemporalPlaceArrangeModels branch.
